title: Ouratea subcordata
taxonomy: Ouratea subcordata belongs to the family Ochthidae. It is a species of plant in the genus Ouratea. Ouratea subcordata is further classified into the order Malpighiales and the clade Saxifragidae. The family Ochthidae is a part of the superfamily Celastrales. Ouratea subcordata's classification is also related to the class Magnoliopsida and the clade Rosidae.

intro: Ouratea subcordata is a plant species commonly known as the "Amazon rainforest tree," belonging to the family Ochnaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Amazon region, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Ouratea subcordata include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ouratea subcordata is typically prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to address different health concerns, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
